enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request asking whether he would use teleportation if it destroyed the original and reconstructed a copy. He argues the soul is indestructible and inseparable from the self, so teleportation would preserve identity. He also connects teleportation to alien travel, predicts time travel as the next step, and argues that societal openness and trust — including open borders — must come before advanced technology can emerge.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: would use it — the soul is indestructible and stays with the body through the process, so the destination person is still you
the most powerful and dense thing in the universe; cannot be destroyed; carries all memories permanently
aliens already teleport — faster-than-light physical travel is impossible so they must use wormholes or teleportation
coming after teleportation; 'gods' in mythology and the Bible were likely time-traveling future humans
dismisses them as stupid people who parrot talking points and choose disbelief out of anger, not reason
technology manifests when society is open-minded and trusting enough to be ready for it
necessary prerequisite — closing borders reflects the distrust that blocks technological and civilizational progress
would not be an early beta tester but would use it once widely adopted, to avoid missing out on life

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: I wanted to know whether you would use teleportation if it was ever invented.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: If teleportation worked by copying all your cells and reproducing them at the final destination, would this still be you to your friends and family, to the person at the destination?

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: I wasn't sure if it was possible to transfer a soul through the teleportation process.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: I wouldn't want to teleport if it meant my personal consciousness was erased.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: There would be no way to prove one way or another if the duplicate is you or a copy of you.

claims.subject: human cells
claims.claim: I've read that our human cells are replaced every few years, so we aren't even the same person we used to be.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: Would you feel confident enough to try teleportation?

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: Would you be accepting of a lower quality of life than everyone else in society if you didn't teleport?

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: I don't know if I would be able to teleport.

claims.subject: aliens
claims.claim: If you believe in aliens, how do you think they travel around?

claims.subject: space travel
claims.claim: They can't fly like we fly from here to Mars; they'd crash into little asteroids and it would take too long.

claims.subject: space travel
claims.claim: The closest star to here is four and a half light years away, and traveling at the speed of light would still take four and a half years.

claims.subject: aliens
claims.claim: Aliens travel here for vacations or short trips, so they must already know how to teleport.

claims.subject: space travel
claims.claim: If you can imagine traveling to other stars or galaxies, then teleportation is how you would do it.

claims.subject: human soul
claims.claim: The human soul is designed to grow, and the human experience is designed to grow.

claims.subject: society
claims.claim: If you can imagine something happening and feel like it's the natural progress of our society, then it is the natural progress of our society.

claims.subject: belief
claims.claim: You think of it, and then you do it, and anybody who tells you otherwise is just a grumpy, angry, stupid person.

claims.subject: skeptics
claims.claim: Skeptics and atheists are just dumb people who grew up in a society where everyone around them chooses to disbelieve.

claims.subject: belief
claims.claim: People who disbelieve don't have reasons; they just spout nonsense talking points they hear.

claims.subject: imagination
claims.claim: If you can imagine traveling to another star, then you can travel to another star.

claims.subject: science
claims.claim: Stupid people in politics and society believe in science selectively to back up their stupid ideas.

claims.subject: paradigm
claims.claim: If you have a paradigm that is open-minded and offers a way to grow, then by default, that's the correct paradigm.

claims.subject: facts
claims.claim: All scientific data will support an open-minded paradigm if you believe in it.

claims.subject: alchemy
claims.claim: Alchemy and early chemistry were based on imagination, and now we're in a similar stage with teleportation.

claims.subject: imagination
claims.claim: The golden rule of imagination is that anything you imagine is possible.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: We should believe in teleportation now, even if we don't know how it happens.

claims.subject: time travel
claims.claim: Teleportation and time travel are the things we've got to do, and they're kind of the same thing.

claims.subject: time travel
claims.claim: Time travel is like version 2.0 of the teleportation machine.

claims.subject: time travel
claims.claim: When you time travel, you're going to another reality in a different time period.

claims.subject: future society
claims.claim: By the time we can time travel, society will be utopian enough to prevent people from messing things up.

claims.subject: aliens
claims.claim: Aliens or future humans time-traveling to the past are where the gods in the Bible and Greek mythology come from.

claims.subject: technology
claims.claim: How much technology we have depends on how open-minded we are as a society.

claims.subject: society
claims.claim: If we open all borders and stop racism, we could shift industry from war to transportation and tourism.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: If you make society ready for teleportation machines, the technology will magically appear or be given to us by aliens.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: You would know it's you teleporting because how could it not be you?

claims.subject: human consciousness
claims.claim: Yes, I would use teleportation, but I wouldn't be the first to test it.

claims.subject: teleportation technology
claims.claim: The technology for teleportation is actually pretty simple once you understand it in the right way.

claims.subject: nanotechnology
claims.claim: We're developing micro-machines that can collect molecules and create things, which could help with teleportation.

claims.subject: DNA
claims.claim: Once we map the DNA of a human being, we can create human beings.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: Your soul would know what's happening during teleportation and wouldn't detach from your body.

claims.subject: organ transplants
claims.claim: When people get organ transplants, their soul just attaches to the new organ, and it works the same way.

claims.subject: human body
claims.claim: Our human bodies are like machines, and if you engineer them to fit with technology, they will work.

claims.subject: human body
claims.claim: Human bodies follow the same scientific laws as computers; we're all electromagnetic devices.

claims.subject: brain waves
claims.claim: The brain wave frequency of our brains overlaps with the Earth's frequency, which is about 8 Hz.

claims.subject: mind control
claims.claim: You can shut people's brains off or control them the same way you can with machines.

claims.subject: soul
claims.claim: Your soul is the most dense, powerful thing in the universe and cannot be destroyed.

claims.subject: soul
claims.claim: The soul is the source point of energy, and everything else comes from it.

claims.subject: memories
claims.claim: Nothing can erase your memories because they are part of your soul.

claims.subject: God
claims.claim: When Christians say God is watching you, it's really you watching yourself.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: You can prove a duplicate is you the same way you prove it's you on the phone—by asking personal questions.

claims.subject: fear
claims.claim: People freak out and get scared for no reason, like with Ouija boards or teleportation.

claims.subject: teleportation
claims.claim: I would teleport if everybody else did it, because otherwise I'd feel like an idiot afraid to experience life.

claims.subject: society
claims.claim: To use advanced technology, society must first learn to trust and not be racist or prejudiced.

claims.subject: trust
claims.claim: It's better to be the person who gets fucked over than the one who fucks others over.

claims.subject: borders
claims.claim: Opening up borders is the key to advancing society and technology.

claims.subject: Canada
claims.claim: Canada wouldn't even let me visit for a day because they didn't trust me, and that's the kind of thing we need to cut out.

claims.subject: technology
claims.claim: If we cut out distrust and prejudice, we can use advanced technology; if not, we can't.
